title: Nunnezharia lepidota
taxonomy: Nunnezharia lepidota is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, commonly known as the buckthorn or dogwood family. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. The plant is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the states of Arizona, New Mexico, and Texas. Nunnezharia lepidota is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 2 meters tall, with small white flowers and elliptical leaves. The species is relatively rare and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas.
